title: malaxis woodsonii
intro: Malaxis woodsonii, also known as the Wood's adder's-mouth orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Malaxis woodsonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and treat various infections, such as urinary tract infections and skin conditions like eczema. Malaxis woodsonii can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or topical ointment for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
